POINTS:
- [tycho-core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2/publisher/AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:113] In fact the currently used method also makes the created requirement greedy:
https://github.com/eclipse-equinox/p2/blob/5b57f58f9ad96c332dcf99218b24dbc5a5cfb705/bundles/org.eclipse.equinox.p2.metadata/src/org/eclipse/equinox/p2/metadata/MetadataFactory.java#L543C39-L543C43

So this is already as desired.
v7:
SUMMARY:
This PR makes `jars.extra.classpath` platform URL entries optional (soft) requirements instead of required (hard) requirements, aligning Tycho's behavior with PDE, which silently ignores unresolvable entries. The single-line change from `false` to `true` in the `optional` parameter of `MetadataFactory.createRequirement` is correct and minimal.

POINTS:
- [tycho-core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2/publisher/AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:112-113] The change is semantically correct: making the `optional` flag `true` means p2 will not fail resolution if a platform-specific bundle (e.g., `org.eclipse.swt.cocoa.macosx.aarch64` when building on Linux) cannot be found. This matches PDE's behavior of ignoring unresolvable entries. The pattern is consistent with Tycho's existing optional requirements (see `InstallableUnitResolver.java:225` for source bundles and `P2ResolverImpl.java:363` for the `optionalGreedyRequirementTo` helper).

- [tycho-core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2/publisher/AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:91-101] Note that this change affects both `jars.extra.classpath` (global) and `extra.<jar>=...` (per-jar) entries, since both flow through `extractExtraEntriesAsIURequirement()`. This is consistent behavior — platform-specific per-jar extra entries should also be optional.

- [tycho-its/src/test/java/.../TYCHO253extraClassPathEntries/] Consider adding an integration test for the unresolvable scenario. Existing tests only verify that resolvable extra classpath entries work correctly. A test with a platform-specific fragment or non-existent bundle in `jars.extra.classpath` would validate that resolution succeeds without error.

- [tycho-core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2/publisher/AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:38-44] Minor: the import reordering (moving `OptionalResolutionAction` before `StatusTool`) is a cosmetic improvement that maintains alphabetical order.
v8:
SUMMARY:
This PR changes the `optional` flag from `false` to `true` when creating IU requirements for `jars.extra.classpath` platform URLs, allowing Tycho to gracefully ignore unresolvable platform-specific bundles (e.g., fragments for other OS/arch combinations), matching PDE behavior.

POINTS:
- [tycho-core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2/publisher/AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:112-113] The change from `false` to `true` (5th parameter) correctly sets the requirement as optional. This is confirmed by multiple codebase conventions: `ArtifactTypeHelper.java:93` has an explicit comment `// optional=false, multiple=true` documenting the same 6-arg overload, `P2ResolverImpl.java:363` names its helper `optionalGreedyRequirementTo` and passes `true` in this position, and `InstallableUnitResolver.java:225` uses a variable named `optionalGreedySourceBundleRequirement` with `true` here. The change is semantically correct and well-justified.
- [tycho-its/src/test/java/org/eclipse/tycho/test/TYCHO253extraClassPathEntries/ExtraClassPathEntriesTest.java] No existing integration test covers the scenario where a `jars.extra.classpath` entry references an unresolvable bundle. The existing tests all assume referenced bundles are available in the target platform. Consider adding a test case that references a platform-specific bundle not available in the test environment to validate the optional resolution behavior — this would guard against regression and document the intended behavior.
- [tycho-core/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2/publisher/AbstractMetadataGenerator.java:38-47] Minor: the diff includes a cosmetic reordering of the `StatusTool` import. Not functional, but worth noting for reviewers.
